step_by_step_thinking:
Given the description of a child whose mother is very indulgent towards him/her, we might see traits indicative of someone with either oppositional defiant disorder (ODD), intermittent explosive disorder (IED), or even certain aspects of conduct disorder (CD).
Indulgence from a maternal figure could result in a child developing problematic behaviors where they don't adhere strictly to rules and routines. This kind of upbringing fosters an environment where the child doesn’t learn delayed gratification nor respect boundaries — characteristics closely linked with ODD. Children under such circumstances would be prone to defiance and arguments, fitting neatly into the symptomatology of ODD.
Option B suggests aggressive displays similar to what might arise in situations involving inappropriate parenting practices, specifically highlighting temper tantrums which align with descriptions found among children suffering from ODD.
Thus, considering the context given and matching patterns described in literature related to both ODD and excessive parental lenience, my response leans towards \boxed{B}. An aggressively displaying temperament fits well with scenarios arising from highly indulgent upbringings according to referenced studies.
